heading: Merit system principles and civil service protections: applicability
label: 1612 Merit system principles and civil service protections: applicability
notes: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1996 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 1996section 1635 of Pub. L. 104–201section 1593 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1996 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 1996section 1635 of Pub. L. 104–201section 1593 of this title

children.heading: .—Applicability of Merit System Principles
children.label: a .—Applicability of Merit System Principles
children.text: shall apply to the exercise of authority under this subchapter (other than sections 1605 and 1611).Section 2301 of title 5

If, in the case of a position established under authority other than that is reestablished as an excepted service position under that section, the provisions of law referred to in paragraph (2) applied to the person serving in that position immediately before the position is so reestablished and such provisions of law would not otherwise apply to the person while serving in the position as so reestablished, then such provisions of law shall, subject to paragraph (3), continue to apply to the person with respect to service in that position for as long as the person continues to serve in the position without a break in service.section 1601(a)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Section 2302, relating to prohibited personnel practices.

children.label: A
children.text: Notwithstanding any provision of chapter 75 of title 5, an appeal of an adverse action by an individual employee covered by paragraph (1) shall be determined within the Department of Defense if the employee so elects.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall prescribe the procedures for initiating and determining appeals of adverse actions pursuant to elections made under subparagraph (A).
